what: Tools for Multilayer and Single Layer Network Modeling
title: Tools for Multilayer and Single Layer Network Modeling
text: MixMashNet R package provides unified framework estimating analysing single layer multilayer networks using Mixed Graphical Models (MGMs). package designed analysis complex biomedical epidemiological data, allowing joint modelling conditional dependencies variables mixed types (continuous, binary, categorical), within across multiple layers.

title: Tools for Multilayer and Single Layer Network Modeling
text: MixMashNet provides tools : estimation MGM networks; specification estimation multilayer network structures; bootstrap-based stability edges node-level indices; computation centrality bridge metrics; assessment membership stability community scores; visualization networks associated metrics, including interactive exploration via Shiny applications.

title: Tools for Multilayer and Single Layer Network Modeling
text: MixMashNet can installed CRAN : latest version can installed GitHub :
code: install.packages("MixMashNet") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("ARCbiostat/MixMashNet")

what: Tools for Multilayer and Single Layer Network Modeling — MixMashNet-package
title: Tools for Multilayer and Single Layer Network Modeling — MixMashNet-package
text: Tools estimating analyzing single layer multilayer networks using Mixed Graphical Models (MGMs), accommodating continuous, count, categorical variables. multilayer setting, layers may comprise different types numbers variables, users can explicitly impose predefined multilayer topology constrain estimation inter intralayer connections. package implements bootstrap procedures derive quantile regions edge weights node-level centrality bridge metrics, provides tools assess stability node community membership. addition, subject-level community scores can computed summarize latent dimensions identified network clustering.

title: Tools for Multilayer and Single Layer Network Modeling — MixMashNet-package
text: De Martino, M., Triolo, F., Perigord, ., Ornago, . M., Vetrano, D. L., Gregorio, C. (2026). MixMashNet: R Package Single Multilayer Networks. https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.05716 Christensen, . P., & Golino, H. (2021). Estimating Stability Psychological Dimensions via Bootstrap Exploratory Graph Analysis: Monte Carlo Simulation Tutorial. Psych, 3(3), 479–500. doi:10.3390/psych3030032 Christensen, . P., Golino, H., Abad, F. J., & Garrido, L. E. (2025). Revised network loadings. Behavior Research Methods, 57(4), 114. doi:10.3758/s13428-025-02640-3 Epskamp, S., Borsboom, D., & Fried, E. . (2018). Estimating psychological networks accuracy: tutorial paper. Behavior Research Methods, 50(1), 195–212. doi:10.3758/s13428-017-0862-1 Haslbeck, J. M. B., & Waldorp, L. J. (2020). mgm: Estimating Time-Varying Mixed Graphical Models High-Dimensional Data. Journal Statistical Software, 93(8). doi:10.18637/jss.v093.i08 Jones, P. J., Ma, R., & McNally, R. J. (2021). Bridge Centrality: Network Approach Understanding Comorbidity. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 56(2), 353–367. doi:10.1080/00273171.2019.1614898

what: Safe graph assembly for mgm outputs (modified from mgm) — Reg2Graph_safe
title: Safe graph assembly for mgm outputs (modified from mgm) — Reg2Graph_safe
text: Robust post-processing assemble pairwise (nodewise) weighted adjacency matrices mgm-style object. variant tolerates masked designs empty parameter blocks using conservative fallbacks na.rm = TRUE appropriate. preserves structure original mgm output.

what: Bacteremia example dataset for single layer network analysis — bacteremia
title: Bacteremia example dataset for single layer network analysis — bacteremia
text: Clinical bacteremia dataset used illustrate single-layer network estimation MixMashNet dataset contains 7240 patients clinical suspicion bacteremia underwent blood culture testing Vienna General Hospital.

title: Bacteremia example dataset for single layer network analysis — bacteremia
code: data(bacteremia)

title: Bacteremia example dataset for single layer network analysis — bacteremia
text: data frame 7420 rows 16 variables: AGE Age (numeric). WBC White blood cell (numeric). NEU Neutrophil counts (numeric). HGB Hemoglobin (numeric). PLT Platelet count (numeric). CRP C-reactive protein (numeric). APTT Activated partial thromboplastin time (numeric). FIB Fibrinogen (numeric). CREA Creatinine (numeric). BUN Blood urea nitrogen (numeric). GLU Glucose (numeric). ALAT High-sensitivity C-reactive protein (numeric). GBIL Total bilirubin (numeric). ALB Albumin (numeric). SEX Sex 0=male 1=female. BloodCulture Positive blood culture results 0=1=yes.

title: Bacteremia example dataset for single layer network analysis — bacteremia
text: Ratzinger, F., Dedeyan, M., Rammerstorfer, M., Perkmann, T., Burgmann, H., Makristathis, ., Dorffner, G., Loetsch, F., Blacky, ., & Ramharter, M. (2014). risk prediction model screening bacteremic patients: cross sectional study. PLoS ONE, 9(9), e106765. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0106765

what: Bridge metrics for nodes across communities — bridge_metrics
title: Bridge metrics for nodes across communities — bridge_metrics
text: Computes bridge centrality measures nodes assigned community. function used internally mixMN() multimixMN(). Specifically, function computes bridge strength sum absolute edge weights connecting node nodes communities; bridge expected influence order one (EI1) signed sum direct connections nodes communities; bridge expected influence order two (EI2) signed influence propagates indirectly nodes communities via one intermediate neighbor (.e., paths length two); bridge betweenness number times node lies shortest paths nodes belonging different communities; bridge closeness inverse mean shortest-path distance nodes communities.

title: Bridge metrics for nodes across communities — bridge_metrics
code: bridge_metrics(g, membership)

title: Bridge metrics for nodes across communities — bridge_metrics
text: g igraph object edge attribute weight. membership Named vector/factor community labels subset nodes (names must match V(g)$name).

title: Bridge metrics for nodes across communities — bridge_metrics
text: data.frame columns: node, community, bridge_strength, bridge_ei1,        bridge_ei2, bridge_betweenness, bridge_closeness.

title: Bridge metrics for nodes across communities — bridge_metrics
text: Bridge betweenness closeness computed positive-weight subgraph , weights converted distances \(d = 1/w\).

title: Bridge metrics for nodes across communities — bridge_metrics
text: Jones, P. J., Ma, R., & McNally, R. J. (2021). Bridge Centrality: Network Approach Understanding Comorbidity. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 56(2), 353–367. doi:10.1080/00273171.2019.1614898

code: bridge_metrics_excluded(g, membership)

title: Bridge metrics for nodes excluded from communities — bridge_metrics_excluded
text: data.frame columns: node, bridge_strength, bridge_closeness, bridge_betweenness,         bridge_ei1, bridge_ei2.

title: Bridge metrics for nodes excluded from communities — bridge_metrics_excluded
text: Bridge betweenness excluded closeness excluded computed positive-weight subgraph , weights converted distances \(d = 1/w\).

title: Bridge metrics for nodes excluded from communities — bridge_metrics_excluded

what: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
title: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
text: Computes subject-level community scores. Community scores obtained weighted sums variables belonging detected community, weights correspond standardized community loadings estimated via EGAnet::net.loads stored fitted mixMN_fit object. Scores computed using dataset provided via data argument. data = NULL, original dataset used fit model (fit$model$data) used default. Optionally, percentile bootstrap quantile regions community scores can computed bootstrap community loadings available fit$community_loadings$boot. Community scores available community loadings computed fitted model. requires variables community subgraph MGM type Gaussian ("g"), Poisson ("p"), binary categorical ("c" level == 2).

title: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
code: community_scores(   fit,   data = NULL,   layer = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   quantile_level = NULL,   return_quantile_region = FALSE,   na_action = c("stop", "omit") )

title: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
text: fit fitted object class c("mixMN_fit", "multimixMN_fit") returned mixMN() multimixMN(). data Optional data.frame variables columns. NULL, uses fit$model$data. Errors NULL. layer Optional. fit multimixMN_fit, specify layer score (name index). NULL, scores computed scoreable layers returned named list. layer scoreable, function errors. scale Logical; TRUE (default), z-standardize variables used scoring, using mean/SD computed dataset used scoring. quantile_level Optional numeric 0 1, e.g. 0.95 0.99. provided, percentile bootstrap quantile regions computed community scores (requires fit$community_loadings$boot). return_quantile_region Logical; TRUE, return quantile regions. na_action Character. handle missing values scoring data: "stop" (default) stops missing value present required variables; "omit" computes scores using row-wise omission within community (.e., uses available variables , re-normalizing weights within community row).

title: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
text: list class "community_scores" containing: call matched call. settings List scale, quantile_level, na_action. ids Character vector subject IDs (rownames data). communities Character vector community score names. scores Numeric matrix scores (n × K). quantile_region requested available, list lower upper     matrices (n × K) percentile bootstrap quantile regions; otherwise NULL. details List containing nodes_used, loadings_true,     loadings_boot_available, scaling parameters (center, scale). fit mixMN_fit (multimixMN_fit layer specified), returns "community_scores" object. fit multimixMN_fit layer = NULL, returns named list community_scores objects scoreable layers. layer scoreable, function errors.

title: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
text: function requires fit$community_loadings$true exists input data contains required variables fit$community_loadings$nodes. errors otherwise.

title: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
text: Christensen, . P., Golino, H., Abad, F. J., & Garrido, L. E. (2025). Revised network loadings. Behavior Research Methods, 57(4), 114. doi:10.3758/s13428-025-02640-3

title: Compute community scores from a fitted MixMashNet model — community_scores
code: data(bacteremia)  vars <- c("WBC", "NEU", "HGB", "PLT", "CRP") df <- bacteremia[, vars]  fit <- mixMN(   data = df,   lambdaSel = "EBIC",   reps = 0,   seed_model = 42,   compute_loadings = TRUE,   progress = FALSE,   save_data = TRUE )  # Compute community scores on the original data scores <- community_scores(fit) summary(scores) #> Summary of MixMashNet community scores #> ======================================== #>  #> Subjects:    7420 #> Communities: 2 #>  #> Settings #>   Scaling:   TRUE #>   NA action: stop #>   Quantile region: not computed #>  #> Per-community statistics (across subjects) #>  community       mean     sd   median      q1     q3    min    max #>          1 -2.454e-16 1.6180 -0.25530 -1.0470 0.7385 -2.864 20.130 #>          2  1.321e-17 0.7288 -0.01143 -0.5287 0.5054 -2.032  3.173

what: Bridge profiles of a node across communities — find_bridge_communities
title: Bridge profiles of a node across communities — find_bridge_communities
text: Identifies communities contribute bridge role given node, decomposing bridge connectivity community-specific contributions, excluding community assigned. function designed interpretative companion bridge_metrics() bridge_metrics_excluded(), providing components underlying corresponding overall bridge indices. Bridge connectivity summarized using five complementary profiles: bridge strength, bridge EI1, bridge EI2, bridge closeness, bridge betweenness. single layer fits (mixMN_fit), profiles computed directly supplied fitted object. multilayer fits (multimixMN_fit), profiles computed within selected layer , applying single layer procedure corresponding intralayer fit stored fit$layer_fits[[layer]].

title: Bridge profiles of a node across communities — find_bridge_communities
code: find_bridge_communities(fit, node, layer = NULL)

title: Bridge profiles of a node across communities — find_bridge_communities
text: fit object class mixMN_fit multimixMN_fit. node Character scalar: node interest. layer Character scalar giving layer interest multimixMN_fit objects. Ignored mixMN_fit objects.

title: Bridge profiles of a node across communities — find_bridge_communities
text: object class "bridge_profiles" (named list)   following components: bridge_strength Bridge strength. List overall, total     value across communities, by_comm, tibble     community-specific contributions (community, sum_abs_w). bridge_ei1 Bridge expected influence (order 1). List     overall by_comm (community, sum_signed_w). bridge_ei2 Bridge expected influence (order 2). List     overall by_comm (community, sum_signed_w2). bridge_closeness Bridge closeness. List overall     by_comm (community, inv_mean_dist). bridge_betweenness Bridge betweenness. List overall     by_pair, tibble contributions community pair     (Ci, Cj, hits).

title: Bridge profiles of a node across communities — find_bridge_communities
text: Bridge profiles computed using connections focal node nodes communities different . focal node assigned community, .e. excluded, connections assigned nodes communities considered. Bridge betweenness computed counting shortest paths pairs nodes different communities pass focal node intermediate vertex. multiple shortest paths exist, path counted separately.

what: Bridge profiles of a node across layers — find_bridge_layers
title: Bridge profiles of a node across layers — find_bridge_layers
text: Identifies layers contribute interlayer bridge role given node, decomposing interlayer connectivity layer-specific contributions. function designed interpretative companion interlayer node-level indices returned multimixMN(), providing components underlying corresponding overall interlayer indices. Interlayer connectivity summarized using four complementary profiles: interlayer strength, interlayer expected influence (order 1), interlayer closeness, interlayer betweenness.

title: Bridge profiles of a node across layers — find_bridge_layers
code: find_bridge_layers(fit, node, layer)

title: Bridge profiles of a node across layers — find_bridge_layers
text: fit object class multimixMN_fit. node Character scalar: node interest. layer Character scalar giving layer focal node.

title: Bridge profiles of a node across layers — find_bridge_layers
text: object class "bridge_layer_profiles" (named list)   following components: bridge_strength List overall by_layer,   by_layer tibble columns   target_layer sum_abs_w. bridge_ei1 List overall by_layer,   by_layer tibble columns   target_layer sum_signed_w. bridge_closeness List overall   by_layer, by_layer tibble columns   target_layer contribution. bridge_betweenness List overall   by_pair, by_pair tibble columns   Li, Lj, contribution.

title: Bridge profiles of a node across layers — find_bridge_layers
text: function operates interlayer-graph, .e. graph containing edges nodes belonging different layers. focal node selected layer, function decomposes: interlayer strength contributions toward layer; interlayer expected influence (order 1) signed contributions         toward layer; interlayer closeness additive harmonic-distance contributions         toward layer; interlayer betweenness additive contributions shortest         paths layer pairs, using standard fraction         \(\sigma_{st}(v) / \sigma_{st}\). Contributions defined sum corresponding overall interlayer index.

title: Extract node-level centrality indices — get_centrality
text: Extracts node-level centrality indices fitted objects returned mixMN() multimixMN() long-format data frame. single layer fits class "mixMN_fit", intralayer node-level indices available. multilayer fits class "multimixMN_fit", = "intra" returns intralayer node-level indices, whereas = "inter" returns node-level indices computed interlayer-graph. specified multilayer fit, intralayer interlayer node-level indices returned default, unless layer pairs imply specific scope. function returns original estimates , available, bootstrap means, standard errors, bootstrap quantile regions.

title: Extract node-level centrality indices — get_centrality
code: get_centrality(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'mixMN_fit' get_centrality(   object,   what = "intra",   statistics = NULL,   digits = NULL,   drop_na_boot = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'multimixMN_fit' get_centrality(   object,   what = c("intra", "inter"),   statistics = NULL,   layer = NULL,   pairs = NULL,   digits = NULL,   drop_na_boot = TRUE,   ... )

title: Extract node-level centrality indices — get_centrality
text: object fitted object class "mixMN_fit" "multimixMN_fit". ... arguments passed methods. Character string indicating node-level indices extract: "intra": intralayer node-level indices; "inter": interlayer-node-level indices     (available "multimixMN_fit" objects). single layer fits, "intra" allowed. multilayer fits, omitted, scopes returned default unless layer pairs imply specific scope. statistics Character vector specifying node-level statistics include. digits Optional number digits used round numeric columns. drop_na_boot Logical. TRUE (default), bootstrap-related columns entirely NA removed output. layer Optional character vector layer names subset. Relevant intralayer output multilayer fits. pairs Optional character vector layer-pair names subset. Relevant interlayer output multilayer fits.

title: Extract node-level centrality indices — get_centrality
text: tibble long format one row per node-statistic combination. contains columns: node layer scope metric estimated available, output also contains bootstrap summary columns: mean.bootstrap SE.bootstrap quantile.lower.bootstrap quantile.upper.bootstrap quantile level used compute bootstrap quantile region stored "quantile_level" attribute returned tibble.

title: Extract node-level centrality indices — get_centrality
text: returned data frame long format, one row per node-statistic combination. single layer fits, = "intra" available. multilayer fits, layer can used subset intralayer output, whereas pairs can used subset interlayer output. set admissible statistics depends class object. particular, bridge-related indices available intralayer output.

title: Extract edge-level summaries — get_edges
text: Extracts edge-level summaries fitted objects returned mixMN() multimixMN() long-format data frame. single layer fits class "mixMN_fit", intralayer edges available. multilayer fits class "multimixMN_fit", = "intra" returns intralayer edges, whereas = "inter" returns interlayer edges. specified multilayer fit, scopes returned default, unless layer pairs imply specific scope. function returns original edge weights , available, bootstrap means, standard errors, bootstrap quantile regions.

title: Extract edge-level summaries — get_edges
code: get_edges(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'mixMN_fit' get_edges(object, what = "intra", digits = NULL, drop_na_boot = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'multimixMN_fit' get_edges(   object,   what = c("intra", "inter"),   layer = NULL,   pairs = NULL,   digits = NULL,   drop_na_boot = TRUE,   ... )

title: Extract edge-level summaries — get_edges

Character string indicating edge-level summaries extract: "intra": intralayer edges; "inter": interlayer edges (available     "multimixMN_fit" objects). single layer fits, "intra" allowed. multilayer fits, omitted, scopes returned default unless layer pairs imply specific scope. digits Optional number digits used round numeric columns. drop_na_boot Logical. TRUE (default), bootstrap-related columns entirely NA removed output. layer Optional character vector layer names subset. Relevant intralayer output multilayer fits. pairs Optional character vector layer-pair names subset. Relevant interlayer output multilayer fits.

title: Extract edge-level summaries — get_edges
text: tibble long format one row per edge. contains columns: edge layer intralayer edges pairs interlayer edges scope estimated available, output also contains bootstrap summary columns: mean.bootstrap SE.bootstrap quantile.lower.bootstrap quantile.upper.bootstrap quantile level used compute bootstrap quantile region stored "quantile_level" attribute returned tibble.

title: Extract edge-level summaries — get_edges
text: returned data frame long format, one row per edge. single layer fits, = "intra" available. multilayer fits, layer can used subset intralayer output, whereas pairs can used subset interlayer output.

what: Node stability from bootstrap community assignments — membershipStab
title: Node stability from bootstrap community assignments — membershipStab
text: Computes per-node stability given empirical community structure homogenized bootstrap memberships contained mixMN_fit object. Stability expressed proportion bootstrap replications assign node empirical (original) community.

title: Node stability from bootstrap community assignments — membershipStab
code: membershipStab(fit)

title: Node stability from bootstrap community assignments — membershipStab
text: fit object returned mixMN() (class mixMN_fit), containing $communities$original_membership $communities$boot_memberships. Bootstrap memberships must available, .e. reps > 0 "community" %% boot_what.

title: Node stability from bootstrap community assignments — membershipStab
text: object class c("membershipStab"), components: membership List : empirical Named integer vector empirical community labels bootstrap Matrix homogenized bootstrap labels         (reps × p)  membership.stability List : empirical.dimensions Named numeric vector node-level stability         (proportion assigned empirical community) .dimensions Matrix (p × K) proportions         assignment community  community_palette Named vector colors communities,     available

title: Node stability from bootstrap community assignments — membershipStab
text: Bootstrap community labels first aligned empirical solution using EGAnet::community.homogenize(). Stability computed node-wise proportion bootstrap runs node's community matches empirical assignment.

title: Node stability from bootstrap community assignments — membershipStab
text: Christensen, . P., & Golino, H. (2021). Estimating Stability Psychological Dimensions via Bootstrap Exploratory Graph Analysis: Monte Carlo Simulation Tutorial. Psych, 3(3), 479–500. doi:10.3390/psych3030032

title: Masked MGM (modified from mgm) — mgm_masked
text: modified version model-fitting routine mgm adds support per-node predictor mask via mask_list. provided, node estimated using allowed predictors specified node. functionality mirrors original mgm nodewise estimation graph assembly.

title: Masked MGM (modified from mgm) — mgm_masked
code: mgm_masked(   data,   type,   level,   regularize,   lambdaSeq,   lambdaSel,   lambdaFolds,   lambdaGam,   alphaSeq,   alphaSel,   alphaFolds,   alphaGam,   k,   moderators,   ruleReg,   weights,   threshold,   method,   binarySign,   scale,   verbatim,   pbar,   warnings,   saveModels,   saveData,   overparameterize,   thresholdCat,   signInfo,   mask_list = NULL,   ... )

title: Masked MGM (modified from mgm) — mgm_masked
text: data Numeric matrix (n × p). missing values allowed. type Character vector length p variable types mgm (e.g., "g" Gaussian, "c" categorical, "p" Poisson). level Integer vector length p variable levels mgm. regularize Logical; FALSE, equivalent regularization (lambdaSel = "EBIC", lambdaSeq = 0, threshold = "none"). lambdaSeq, lambdaSel, lambdaFolds, lambdaGam Lambda grid/selection settings (see mgm). alphaSeq, alphaSel, alphaFolds, alphaGam Mixing parameter grid/selection (see mgm). k Interaction order (default 2); meaning mgm. moderators Optional moderators specification (mgm). ruleReg Regularization rule, default "" (mgm). weights Observation weights (length n); internally normalized. threshold Thresholding rule (default "LW") mgm. method Fitting backend (default "glm") mgm. binarySign Logical; TRUE, store sign information. scale Logical; TRUE, standardize Gaussian variables. verbatim, pbar, warnings, saveModels, saveData Overhead/UX flags mgm. overparameterize Logical; use overparameterized design matrix (mgm). thresholdCat Logical; categorical thresholding (mgm). signInfo Logical; store sign information (mgm). mask_list Optional list length p; element integer vector column indices (1..p) indicating predictors allowed nodewise regression corresponding target node. NULL, masking applied. ... arguments forwarded consistently mgm.

title: Masked MGM (modified from mgm) — mgm_masked
text: object class c("mgm","core") fields analogous mgm: $pairwise$: wadj, signs, nodewise variants. $interactions$, $intercepts$, $nodemodels$: mgm. $call$: input metadata (types, levels, thresholds, etc.).

title: Masked MGM (modified from mgm) — mgm_masked
text: function adapted mgm workflow preserves nodewise estimation, cross-validation / EBIC selection, thresholding, graph assembly. substantive extension mask_list, filters node's design matrix allowed predictors fitting. enables constrained structures (e.g., multilayer masks) keeping mgm’s estimation logic intact.

what: Estimate single layer MGM network with bootstrap centrality, bridge metrics, clustering, and (optionally) community score loadings — mixMN
title: Estimate single layer MGM network with bootstrap centrality, bridge metrics, clustering, and (optionally) community score loadings — mixMN
text: Estimates single layer Mixed Graphical Model (MGM) network original data, using estimation framework implemented mgm package, performs non-parametric bootstrap (row resampling) compute centrality indices, bridge metrics, clustering stability, quantile regions node metrics edge weights. Optionally, function computes community score loadings (later prediction new data) can bootstrap corresponding loadings.

title: Estimate single layer MGM network with bootstrap centrality, bridge metrics, clustering, and (optionally) community score loadings — mixMN
code: mixMN(   data,   reps = 100,   scale = TRUE,   lambdaSel = c("CV", "EBIC"),   lambdaFolds = 5,   lambdaGam = 0.25,   alphaSeq = 1,   alphaSel = "CV",   alphaFolds = 5,   alphaGam = 0.25,   k = 2,   ruleReg = "AND",   threshold = "LW",   overparameterize = FALSE,   thresholdCat = TRUE,   quantile_level = 0.95,   covariates = NULL,   exclude_from_cluster = NULL,   treat_singletons_as_excluded = FALSE,   seed_model = NULL,   seed_boot = NULL,   cluster_method = c("louvain", "edge_betweenness", "fast_greedy", "infomap",     "label_prop", "leading_eigen", "leiden", "optimal", "spinglass", "walktrap"),   cluster_args = list(),   compute_loadings = TRUE,   boot_what = c("general_index", "bridge_index", "excluded_index", "community",     "loadings"),   save_data = FALSE,   progress = TRUE )

title: Estimate single layer MGM network with bootstrap centrality, bridge metrics, clustering, and (optionally) community score loadings — mixMN
text: data data.frame (n x p) variables columns. Variables may numeric, integer, logical, factors. Character Date/POSIXt variables supported must converted prior model fitting. Variable types internally mapped MGM types follows: continuous numeric (double) variables treated Gaussian; integer variables treated Poisson unless take values {0,1}, case treated binary categorical; factors logical variables treated categorical. Binary categorical variables (two-level factors logical variables) internally recoded {0,1} model fitting. original input data modified. reps Integer (>= 0). Number bootstrap replications. scale Logical; TRUE (default) Gaussian variables (type == "g") z-standardized internally mgm(). Use scale = FALSE data already standardized. lambdaSel Method lambda selection: "CV" "EBIC". lambdaFolds Number folds CV (lambdaSel = "CV"). lambdaGam EBIC gamma parameter (lambdaSel = "EBIC"). alphaSeq Alpha parameters elastic net penalty (values 0 1). alphaSel Method selecting alpha parameter: "CV" "EBIC". alphaFolds Number folds CV (alphaSel = "CV"). alphaGam EBIC gamma parameter (alphaSel = "EBIC"). k Integer (>= 1). Order modeled interactions. ruleReg Rule combine neighborhood estimates: "" "". threshold Threshold edge-weights set zero: Available options "LW", "HW", "none". "LW" applies threshold proposed Loh & Wainwright; "HW" applies threshold proposed Haslbeck & Waldorp; "none" disables thresholding. Defaults "LW". overparameterize Logical; controls categorical interactions parameterized neighborhood regressions. TRUE, categorical interactions represented using fully -parameterized design matrix (.e., category combinations explicitly modeled). FALSE, standard glmnet parameterization used, one category serves reference. continuous variables, parameterizations equivalent. default FALSE. -parameterized option may advantageous distinguishing pairwise higher-order interactions. thresholdCat Logical; FALSE thresholds categorical variables set zero. quantile_level Level central bootstrap quantile region (default 0.95). Must single number 0 1. covariates Character vector. Variables used adjustment covariates model estimation. exclude_from_cluster Character vector. Nodes excluded community detection (addition covariates). treat_singletons_as_excluded Logical; TRUE, singleton communities (size 1) treated excluded nodes computing bridge metrics. seed_model Optional integer seed reproducibility initial MGM fit. seed_boot Optional integer seed passed future.apply reproducibility bootstrap replications. cluster_method Community detection method used clustering graph.   Either character string naming one built-methods   "louvain", "edge_betweenness", "fast_greedy",   "infomap", "label_prop", "leading_eigen",   "leiden",  "optimal",   "spinglass", "walktrap",   user-supplied function. function supplied, must accept graph argument   graph return either igraph communities object,   list component membership, membership vector length   equal number nodes used clustering. cluster_args Named list additional arguments passed selected community detection method. example, steps "walktrap", nb.trials "infomap", spins "spinglass". Ignored relevant selected method. compute_loadings Logical; TRUE (default), compute community loadings (EGAnet::net.loads). supported Gaussian, Poisson, binary categorical nodes; otherwise loadings skipped reason stored community_loadings$reason. boot_what Character vector specifying quantities bootstrap. Valid options :  "general_index" (centrality indices),  "bridge_index" (bridge metrics nodes communities),  "excluded_index" (bridge metrics nodes treated excluded),  "community" (community memberships),  "loadings" (community loadings, compute_loadings = TRUE),  "none" (skip node-level bootstrap: edge-weight  bootstrap performed reps > 0). save_data Logical; TRUE, store original data output object. progress Logical; TRUE (default), show bootstrap progress bar.

title: Estimate single layer MGM network with bootstrap centrality, bridge metrics, clustering, and (optionally) community score loadings — mixMN
text: object class "mixMN_fit", list following top-level components: call matched function call. settings List main settings used call, including     reps, cluster_method, cluster_args,     covariates, exclude_from_cluster,     treat_singletons_as_excluded, boot_what. data_info List information derived input data used model setup:     mgm_type_level (data frame one row per variable, reporting     original R class inferred MGM type level,     used call mgm::mgm),     binary_recode_map (named list describing mapping     original binary labels internal {0,1} coding used model fitting). model List :     mgm (fitted mgm object),     nodes (character vector node names),     n (number observations),     p (number variables),     data (save_data = TRUE). graph List describing graph:     igraph (igraph object built     keep_nodes_graph, edge attributes     weight, abs_weight, sign vertex attribute     membership communities),     keep_nodes_graph (nodes retained graph node-level     metrics), keep_nodes_cluster (nodes used community     detection). communities List describing community structure :     original_membership (integer vector community labels     keep_nodes_cluster),     groups (factor community labels actually used bridge     metrics, optionally singletons treated excluded),     palette (named vector colors per community),     boot_memberships (list bootstrap memberships     "community" requested boot_what, otherwise empty     list). statistics List node- edge-level summaries:     node list :     true (data frame one row per node     keep_nodes_graph, containing node name metrics     strength, ei1, closeness, betweenness,     bridge_strength, bridge_betweenness, bridge_closeness,     bridge_ei1, bridge_ei2, nodes treated excluded     communities also     bridge_strength_excluded,     bridge_betweenness_excluded,     bridge_closeness_excluded,     bridge_ei1_excluded, bridge_ei2_excluded);     boot (list bootstrap matrices metric,     dimension reps x length(keep_nodes_graph), possibly NULL     metric requested reps = 0);     quantile_region (list quantile regions node metric, one     p x 2 matrix per metric, columns corresponding lower upper     quantile bounds implied quantile_level, NULL bootstrap performed). edge list :     true (data frame columns edge weight     unique undirected edges among keep_nodes_graph);     boot (matrix bootstrap edge weights dimension     n_edges x reps);     quantile_region (matrix quantile regions edge weights,     n_edges x 2, columns corresponding lower upper     bootstrap quantile bounds, NULL reps = 0). community_loadings List containing community-loading information (based     EGAnet::net.loads) later community-score computation new     data:     nodes(nodes used loadings),     wc (integer community labels aligned nodes),     true (matrix standardized loadings, nodes x communities,     NULL loadings computed.),     boot (list bootstrap loading matrices, one per replication,     NULL bootstrapped),     available (logical indicating whether loadings computed),     reason (character string explaining loadings computed,         NULL available = TRUE),     non_scorable_nodes (character vector nodes community         subgraph prevented loadings computed (e.g., categorical variables         >2 levels), otherwise empty).

what: Build MixMashNet metrics from a signed weighted adjacency matrix — mixMN_from_wadj
title: Build MixMashNet metrics from a signed weighted adjacency matrix — mixMN_from_wadj
text: Low-level constructor computes clustering, centrality, bridge metrics signed weighted adjacency matrix. returns mixMN_fit object analogous quello prodotto da mixMN(), ma stimato direttamente da wadj_signed invece che da mgm(). arguments reps, seed_boot, boot_what stored $settings compatibility bootstrap-based functions, bootstrap performed inside function.

title: Build MixMashNet metrics from a signed weighted adjacency matrix — mixMN_from_wadj
code: mixMN_from_wadj(   wadj_signed,   nodes,   conf_level = 0.95,   exclude_from_graph = NULL,   exclude_from_cluster = NULL,   cluster_method = c("louvain", "fast_greedy", "infomap", "walktrap", "edge_betweenness"),   reps = 0,   seed_boot = NULL,   treat_singletons_as_excluded = FALSE,   boot_what = c("general_index", "interlayer_index", "bridge_index", "excluded_index",     "community", "community_scores", "none") )

title: Build MixMashNet metrics from a signed weighted adjacency matrix — mixMN_from_wadj
text: wadj_signed Square numeric matrix signed edge weights (rows/columns = nodes). Typically wadj * signs mgm. nodes Character vector node names (must match rownames(wadj_signed) colnames(wadj_signed)). conf_level Confidence level percentile bootstrap CIs (default 0.95). Stored $settings consistency mixMN(). exclude_from_graph Character vector nodes exclude entirely graph (edges, centralities). exclude_from_cluster Character vector nodes exclude clustering (remain graph, community label). cluster_method Community detection algorithm, one c("louvain","fast_greedy","infomap","walktrap","edge_betweenness"). reps Integer; number bootstrap replications. Currently used inside function, saved $settings$reps consistency mixMN() downstream bootstrap utilities. seed_boot Optional integer; bootstrap seed, stored $settings used . treat_singletons_as_excluded Logical; TRUE, communities size 1 treated "excluded" (nodes receive NA community labels). boot_what Character vector specifying quantities meant bootstrapped higher-level functions: can include "general_index", "interlayer_index", "bridge_index", "excluded_index", "community", "community_scores", "none". value stored $settings$boot_what bootstrap computed .

title: Build MixMashNet metrics from a signed weighted adjacency matrix — mixMN_from_wadj
text: object class c("mixmashnet","mixMN_fit") : graph List : igraph (signed network), keep_nodes_graph, keep_nodes_cluster. communities List empirical community structure (original_membership, groups), color palette (palette), empty boot_memberships. statistics Node-level metrics $statistics$node$true (strength, EI1, closeness, betweenness, bridge metrics, including "excluded" versions) edge weights $statistics$edge$true. Bootstrap slots ($statistics$node$boot, $statistics$edge$boot, $statistics$node$ci, $statistics$edge$ci) set NULL. community_scores Empty containers (obj, df, ci, boot) eventual community scores. settings List echoing main arguments (reps, cluster_method, exclude_from_graph, exclude_from_cluster, treat_singletons_as_excluded, boot_what).

title: Build MixMashNet metrics from a signed weighted adjacency matrix — mixMN_from_wadj
text: Centrality indices computed signed matrix wadj_signed (via qgraph::centrality), distances closeness betweenness based \(|w|\) edge length \(1/|w|\). Bridge metrics computed separately absolute signed graphs using bridge_metrics() bridge_metrics_excluded().

what: Multilayer MGM with bootstrap, intra/interlayer metrics, and quantile regions — multimixMN
title: Multilayer MGM with bootstrap, intra/interlayer metrics, and quantile regions — multimixMN
text: Estimates multilayer Mixed Graphical Model (MGM) using estimation framework implemented mgm package, masking scheme enforces cross-layer edges allowed according layer_rules. Within layer, function computes community structure performs non-parametric row-bootstrap obtain node centrality indices, edge weights, bridge metrics, including metrics nodes treated excluded. Optionally, within-layer community loadings can also estimated bootstrapped. function additionally returns interlayer-node metrics summaries cross-layer edge weights.

title: Multilayer MGM with bootstrap, intra/interlayer metrics, and quantile regions — multimixMN
code: multimixMN(   data,   layers,   layer_rules,   scale = TRUE,   reps = 100,   lambdaSel = c("CV", "EBIC"),   lambdaFolds = 5,   lambdaGam = 0.25,   alphaSeq = 1,   alphaSel = "CV",   alphaFolds = 5,   alphaGam = 0.25,   k = 2,   ruleReg = "AND",   threshold = "LW",   overparameterize = FALSE,   thresholdCat = TRUE,   quantile_level = 0.95,   covariates = NULL,   exclude_from_cluster = NULL,   seed_model = NULL,   seed_boot = NULL,   treat_singletons_as_excluded = FALSE,   cluster_method = c("louvain", "edge_betweenness", "fast_greedy", "infomap",     "label_prop", "leading_eigen", "leiden", "optimal", "spinglass", "walktrap"),   cluster_args = list(),   compute_loadings = TRUE,   boot_what = c("general_index", "interlayer_index", "bridge_index", "excluded_index",     "community", "loadings"),   save_data = FALSE,   progress = TRUE )

title: Multilayer MGM with bootstrap, intra/interlayer metrics, and quantile regions — multimixMN
text: data data.frame (n x p) variables columns. Variables may numeric, integer, logical, factors. Character Date/POSIXt variables supported must converted prior model fitting. Variable types internally mapped MGM types follows: continuous numeric (double) variables treated Gaussian; integer variables treated Poisson unless take values {0,1}, case treated binary categorical; factors logical variables treated categorical. Binary categorical variables (two-level factors logical variables) internally recoded {0,1} model fitting. original input data modified. layers named vector (names = variable names) assigning node layer (character factor). Must cover columns data except variables listed covariates (treated adjustment covariates). layer_rules square matrix (L × L), L number layers. Row column names must match layer names. Entries equal TRUE 1 allow cross-layer edges corresponding pair layers, FALSE 0 disallow . matrix symmetrized internally. Diagonal entries ignored (intralayer edges always permitted). scale Logical; TRUE (default) Gaussian variables (type == "g") z-standardized internally mgm(). Use scale = FALSE data already standardized. reps Integer (>= 0). Number bootstrap replications (row resampling replacement). reps = 0, bootstrap performed. lambdaSel Method lambda selection mgm: "CV" "EBIC". lambdaFolds Number folds CV (lambdaSel = "CV"). lambdaGam EBIC gamma parameter (lambdaSel = "EBIC"). alphaSeq Alpha parameters elastic net penalty (values 0 1). alphaSel Method selecting alpha parameter: "CV" "EBIC". alphaFolds Number folds CV (alphaSel = "CV"). alphaGam EBIC gamma parameter (alphaSel = "EBIC"). k Integer (>= 1). Order modeled interactions. ruleReg Rule combine neighborhood estimates: "" "". threshold Threshold edge-weights set zero: Available options "LW", "HW", "none". "LW" applies threshold proposed Loh & Wainwright; "HW" applies threshold proposed Haslbeck & Waldorp; "none" disables thresholding. Defaults "LW". overparameterize Logical; controls categorical interactions parameterized neighborhood regressions. TRUE, categorical interactions represented using fully -parameterized design matrix (.e., category combinations explicitly modeled). FALSE, standard glmnet parameterization used, one category serves reference. continuous variables, parameterizations equivalent. default FALSE. -parameterized option may advantageous distinguishing pairwise higher-order interactions. thresholdCat Logical; FALSE thresholds categorical variables set zero. quantile_level Level central bootstrap quantile region (default 0.95). Must single number 0 1. covariates Character vector. Variables used adjustment covariates model estimation. exclude_from_cluster Character vector node names. Nodes set excluded community detection addition covariates. seed_model Optional integer seed reproducibility initial MGM fit. seed_boot Optional integer seed passed future.apply reproducibility bootstrap replications. treat_singletons_as_excluded Logical; TRUE, singleton communities (size 1) treated "excluded" computing bridge metrics related summaries. cluster_method Community detection method used within layer.   Either character string naming one built-methods   "louvain", "edge_betweenness", "fast_greedy",   "infomap", "label_prop", "leading_eigen",   "leiden", "optimal",   "spinglass", "walktrap",   user-supplied function. function supplied, must accept graph argument   graph return either igraph communities object,   list component membership, membership vector length   equal number nodes used clustering. cluster_args Named list additional arguments passed selected community detection method. example, steps "walktrap", nb.trials "infomap", spins "spinglass". Ignored relevant selected method. compute_loadings Logical; TRUE (default), compute community loadings (EGAnet::net.loads). supported Gaussian, Poisson, binary categorical nodes; otherwise loadings skipped reason stored community_loadings$reason. boot_what Character vector specifying quantities bootstrap. Valid options : "general_index" (intralayer centrality indices), "interlayer_index" (interlayer-node metrics), "bridge_index" (bridge metrics nodes communities), "excluded_index" (bridge metrics nodes treated excluded), "community" (community memberships), "loadings" (within-layer community loadings, compute_loadings = TRUE), "none" (skip node-level bootstrap: edge-weight bootstrap performed reps > 0). save_data Logical; TRUE, store original data output object. progress Logical; TRUE (default), show bootstrap progress bar.

title: Multilayer MGM with bootstrap, intra/interlayer metrics, and quantile regions — multimixMN
text: object class "multimixMN_fit". returned list contains least following components: call matched function call. settings List main settings used call, including    reps, cluster_method, cluster_args, covariates,     exclude_from_cluster, treat_singletons_as_excluded,     boot_what). data_info List information derived input data used model setup:     mgm_type_level (data frame one row per variable, reporting     original R class inferred MGM type level,     used call mgm::mgm),     binary_recode_map (named list describing mapping     original binary labels internal {0,1} coding used model fitting). model List :     mgm (fitted mgm object),     nodes (character vector node names),     n (number observations),     p (number variables),     data (save_data = TRUE)) layers List describing multilayer structure     (assignment nodes layers, layer_rules matrix used color layer palette). layer_fits Named list (one element per layer)     single layer fits, including community structure, node-level statistics,     edge-level statistics, bridge metrics, (optionally) community loadings     bootstrap information. interlayer List collecting interlayer-node metrics   (strength, expected influence, closeness, betweenness, without   bootstrap) cross-layer edge summaries allowed pair   layers. graph List containing global igraph object built   retained nodes (keep_nodes_graph), vertex attributes   name, layer, membership, edge attributes   weight, abs_weight, sign,   type (intra vs inter) layer_pair.

what: Plot method for single layer MixMashNet objects — plot.mixMN_fit
title: Plot method for single layer MixMashNet objects — plot.mixMN_fit
text: Plotting interface objects returned mixMN(). Depending , method can: = "network": plot estimated single-layer network; = "intra": plot node-level metrics edge weights         bootstrap quantile regions level stored object; = "stability": plot node stability within communities         based bootstrap community assignments.

title: Plot method for single layer MixMashNet objects — plot.mixMN_fit

title: Plot method for single layer MixMashNet objects — plot.mixMN_fit
text: x object class "mixMN_fit", returned mixMN(). Type plot produce. One c("network","intra","stability"). ... Additional arguments. Supported arguments depend : see details .

title: Plot method for single layer MixMashNet objects — plot.mixMN_fit
text: != "network", function returns ggplot object. = "network", network plotted directly.

title: Plot method for single layer MixMashNet objects — plot.mixMN_fit
text: Network plots (= "network"): Supported arguments (via ...): color_by Node coloring: c("community","none"). edge_color_by Edge coloring: c("sign","none"). edge_scale Numeric scaling factor edge widths     (multiplied abs(weight)). graphics::plot.igraph arguments e.g., vertex.size,     vertex.label.cex, edge.width, vertex.label.color, etc. Within-network statistics (= "intra"): Plots node-level metrics edge weights bootstrap quantile regions. Supported arguments (via ...): statistics Character vector metrics. Options include:     "strength", "expected_influence", "closeness",     "betweenness", bridge metrics "bridge_strength",     "bridge_ei1", "bridge_ei2", "bridge_closeness",     "bridge_betweenness", excluded bridge metrics     "bridge_strength_excluded", "bridge_ei1_excluded",     "bridge_ei2_excluded", "bridge_closeness_excluded",     "bridge_betweenness_excluded", "edges".     Different metric families mixed call     (e.g., "edges" combined node metrics). ordering Node ordering:     c("value","alphabetical","community"). standardize Logical; TRUE, z-standardize     displayed values within panel. exclude_nodes Optional character vector node names     remove plotting. color_by_community Logical; TRUE, color nodes     community available. edges_top_n Integer; statistics = "edges", keep     top edges absolute weight. title Optional plot title. Community membership stability (= "stability"): Plots node stability community. Supported arguments (via ...): title Plot title. Default:     "Node Stability Community". cutoff Optional numeric threshold \([0,1]\) shown     dashed vertical line. Use NULL hide line. Default: 0.7. quantile region level taken fitted object (x$settings$quantile_level); missing invalid, default 0.95 used.

Supported arguments (via ...): statistics Character vector metrics. Options include:     "strength", "expected_influence", "closeness",     "betweenness", bridge metrics "bridge_strength",     "bridge_ei1", "bridge_ei2", "bridge_closeness",     "bridge_betweenness", excluded bridge metrics     "bridge_strength_excluded", "bridge_ei1_excluded",     "bridge_ei2_excluded", "bridge_closeness_excluded",     "bridge_betweenness_excluded", "edges".     Different metric families mixed call     (e.g., "edges" combined node metrics). ordering Node ordering:     c("value","alphabetical","community"). standardize Logical; TRUE, z-standardize     displayed values within panel. exclude_nodes Optional character vector node names     remove plotting. color_by_community Logical; TRUE, color nodes     community available. edges_top_n Integer; statistics = "edges", keep     top edges absolute weight. title Optional plot title. omitted multiple layers     shown, layer-specific titles added automatically. Interlayer summaries (= "inter"): Plots interlayer node metrics interlayer edge weights bootstrap quantile regions. Supported arguments (via ...): statistics Character vector. Node metrics:     c("strength","expected_influence","closeness","betweenness"),     "edges" interlayer edge weights. Node metrics     "edges" combined. pairs Layer pairs show. Either "*" (available)     character vector pair keys like "bio_dis"     (order-insensitive). edges_top_n Integer; keep top interlayer edges     absolute weight. ordering Ordering within panels:     c("value","alphabetical"). standardize Logical; TRUE, z-standardize values     (node metrics metric, edges pair). exclude_nodes Optional character vector; removes nodes     incident interlayer edges. nodes_layer Optional layer name restrict node metrics     nodes belonging layer. title Optional plot title. Community membership stability (= "stability"): Plots node stability community. layer provided, layer shown. Otherwise, stability plots shown layers. Supported arguments (via ...): title Plot title. Default:     "Node Stability Community". cutoff Optional numeric threshold \([0,1]\) shown     dashed vertical line. Use NULL hide line. Default: 0.7. quantile region level taken fitted object (x$settings$quantile_level); missing invalid, default 0.95 used.

code: # S3 method for class 'mixmashnet' summary(   object,   what = c("intra", "inter"),   statistics = NULL,   layer = NULL,   pairs = NULL,   digits = 3,   ... )

title: Summarize MixMashNet fits (single and multilayer) in long format — summary.mixmashnet
text: object object class "mixmashnet" returned mixMN() multimixMN(). Character string indicating part model summarize: "intra": intralayer quantities (node-level indices /    intralayer edges); "inter": interlayer quantities (node-level indices     interlayer-graph /cross-layer edges; multilayer fits ). statistics Character vector specifying statistics include.   = "intra", valid values :   c("edges",          "strength", "expected_influence", "closeness", "betweenness",          "bridge_strength", "bridge_closeness", "bridge_betweenness",          "bridge_ei1", "bridge_ei2",          "bridge_strength_excluded", "bridge_betweenness_excluded",          "bridge_closeness_excluded", "bridge_ei1_excluded",          "bridge_ei2_excluded"). = "inter", valid values :   c("edges", "strength", "expected_influence", "closeness",          "betweenness"). statistics = NULL, : = "intra", available intralayer statistics       (including "edges") returned; = "inter", available interlayer statistics       (including "edges") returned. layer Optional character vector layer names subset. Used = "intra" multilayer fits. Ignored single layer fits. pairs Optional character vector layer-pair names (e.g. "bio_dis") used = "inter" summarizing interlayer edges. NULL, available layer pairs included. interlayer node indices, pairs can used restrict summary nodes belonging one layers given pair. digits Number digits round numeric summaries. ... used (S3 compatibility).

title: Summarize MixMashNet fits (single and multilayer) in long format — summary.mixmashnet
text: list (class "summary.mixmashnet") four data frames ($index, $edges, $interlayer_index, $interlayer_edges) quantile region used compute bootstrap quantile regions ($quantile_level). Depending statistics, elements may   NULL.

what: Update community and layer color palettes in MixMashNet objects — update_palette
title: Update community and layer color palettes in MixMashNet objects — update_palette
text: Updates color palettes associated communities /layers fitted mixMN_fit multimixMN_fit objects. mixMN_fit objects, community_colors must named character vector specifying colors community labels object$communities$palette. multimixMN_fit objects, community_colors must named list whose elements correspond layer names. element must named character vector specifying colors community labels layer. list may partial, specified layers updated. multimixMN_fit objects, layer_colors updates palette stored object$layers$palette. function replaces colors corresponding provided names, leaving colors unchanged. Unknown layer names, community labels, layer labels ignored warning.

title: Update community and layer color palettes in MixMashNet objects — update_palette
code: update_palette(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'mixMN_fit' update_palette(object, community_colors = NULL, layer_colors = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'multimixMN_fit' update_palette(object, community_colors = NULL, layer_colors = NULL, ...)

title: Update community and layer color palettes in MixMashNet objects — update_palette
text: object object class mixMN_fit multimixMN_fit. ... arguments passed methods. community_colors mixMN_fit objects, optional named   character vector specifying new colors communities. multimixMN_fit objects, optional named list whose names   layer names whose elements named character vectors specifying new   colors communities within layer. layer_colors Optional named character vector specifying new colors layers. applicable multimixMN_fit objects.

title: Update community and layer color palettes in MixMashNet objects — update_palette
text: input object, updated community /layer palettes.

title: Update community and layer color palettes in MixMashNet objects — update_palette
text: single layer fits, community_colors used. multilayer fits: community_colors updates community palettes within   specified layers; layer_colors updates palette layers . multilayer fits, community_colors can partial: layers included list left unchanged.

title: Update community and layer color palettes in MixMashNet objects — update_palette
code: data(bacteremia)  vars <- c("WBC", "NEU", "HGB", "PLT", "CRP") df <- bacteremia[, vars]  fit <- mixMN(   data = df,   lambdaSel = "EBIC",   reps = 0,   seed_model = 42,   compute_loadings = FALSE,   progress = FALSE )  fit$communities$palette #>         1         2  #> "#E16A86" "#00AD9A"   fit2 <- update_palette(   fit,   community_colors = c("1" = "red", "2" = "blue") )  fit2$communities$palette #>      1      2  #>  "red" "blue"   set.seed(1) plot(fit2)

title: MixMashNet 1.0.0
text: Added get_centrality() get_edges() methods mixMN_fit multimixMN_fit, well layer_slice() multimixMN_fit. Added find_bridge_layers() function. Added support mixMN() multimixMN() user-supplied functions cluster_method argument. Added cluster_args argument mixMN() multimixMN(). summaries mixMN() multimixMN() now display top 10 intralayer interlayer edges, respectively

title: MixMashNet 0.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-03

title: MixMashNet 0.6.0
text: Deleted warnings parameter mgm_masked().

title: MixMashNet 0.5.0
text: Added examples mixMN(), multimixNM(), community_scores() update_palette().

title: MixMashNet 0.4.0
text: Added print() summary() community_scores().

title: MixMashNet 0.3.0
text: Simplified interface mixMN() multimixMN() removing type level arguments. Variable types now inferred automatically. Community score computation now restricted gaussian, poisson binary categorical variables.

title: MixMashNet 0.1.0
text: Development version.
